title: campanula sibirica subsp. elatior
intro: Campanula sibirica subsp. elatior, also known as the tall bellflower, is a perennial plant species in the Campanula genus, native to Siberia and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as relieving coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory problems, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Campanula sibirica subsp. elatior include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies for medicinal purposes.
